title: Consciousness, Memory, and the Architecture of Self-Preservation
abstract: Neuroscientific foundations for cognitive prosthetic design

body.text: Third paper in the P31 Labs research trilogy. Synthesizes evidence across cognitive psychology, computational neuroscience, and digital systems architecture to establish theoretical foundations for cognitive prosthetic design. Central finding: the optimal architecture for both biological and digital cognitive systems is loosely coupled autonomy — maximum local sovereignty with asynchronous connections for learning and reality-testing. This directly validates the Delta mesh topology underlying the P31 Labs ecosystem.
This paper bridges the gap between theoretical geometry and practical neuroscience, establishing that cognitive prosthetics must follow the same principles as the Tetrahedron Protocol.
Neuroscientific basis for cognitive prosthetic architecture
Validation of Delta topology through biological and digital evidence
Framework for sovereign, privacy-preserving human-AI collaboration
Integration with existing Tetrahedron Protocol and Genesis Whitepaper
This paper completes the theoretical trilogy, providing the scientific basis for why the P31 Labs approach to cognitive prosthetics is optimal.
